title: append_note success-response envelope
description: Success response shape for the append_note typed MCP tool. The MCP transport wraps this object as { content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify(<this object>) }] } per the project's responseFormat: 'json' convention. Authoritative source is the Zod schema in src/tools/append_note/schema.ts (AppendNoteOutput).

path.description: Vault-relative path of the note that was written. ALWAYS the canonical resolved path regardless of the input locator shape (FR-003 canonicalisation): callers who supplied 'file: "My Note"' receive 'path: "My Note.md"' (or whatever shortform-resolution yields); callers who supplied 'path: "Sandbox/journal.md"' receive that path back; active-mode callers receive the focused note's vault-relative path.

vault.description: Vault display name. In specific mode, the value from input.vault. In active mode, the vault display name of the currently-focused note (resolved via the cohort's FOCUSED_FILE_TEMPLATE eval).

bytes_written.description: Total bytes the wrapper wrote in this call (post-edit file size minus pre-edit file size). For default-separator appends, equals content.length + 0 (FR-006a — no separator inserted) or content.length + sizeof(separator) (FR-006 — separator inserted; 1 byte for LF, 2 bytes for CRLF). For inline opt-in or 0-byte file cases, exactly equals content.length. Useful for caller-side accounting (total-bytes-per-session tracking, rate-limit budgets).

inline.description: Echo of the inline mode that the wrapper actually applied. False for default-separator calls (no 'inline' in input or 'inline: false'); true for 'inline: true' input. Helps callers confirm the intended mode landed and detect schema-version drift.
